title: Kalshi cofounders' favorite users include 'this guy who lives in Kansas' who is their best inflation forecaster
summary:
The brains behind Kalshi say some of their best prediction market makers are "actually random people."
Mansour said there's no single demographic for these "super forecasters," who comprise some of the prediction market giant's most important users.
Out of the group, Mansour and cofounder Luana Lopes Lara have some favorites — including "a guy from Kansas."
"The best inflation forecaster on Kalshi over the last few years is not— none of the institutions or the big-name hedge funds," Mansour said.
The Trump administration has said it will ease up on prediction markets.

title: Business Insider welcomes Ryan Kailath as senior editor on the economy team
summary:
Ryan Kailath joins Business Insider as a Senior Editor on our Economy team and will oversee a team of reporters covering the job market, student loans, and the intersection of culture and the economy.
Ryan has more than a decade of experience covering the economy, business, and the financial forces shaping everyday life.
As an editor at KUOW, he worked across multiple beats and contributed to award-winning stories recognized with Edward R. Murrow Awards.
"Ryan's combination of technical and business chops is the perfect complement to our people-focused economy coverage," said Bartie Scott, deputy editor at Business Insider.
He was a Knight-Bagehot Fellow in Business and Economics Journalism, focused on economics, accounting, and statistics, business analytics, and corporate finance.

title: Swimply's CEO says the app is bringing the gig economy to pools — and helping cash-strapped homeowners make money
summary:
The gig economy is coming for your backyard pool.
The app lets owners list their pools, where potential users can then book them by the hour.
"Now, they have a more epic backyard, and start charging more and gaining more traction for their pool," Laskin said.
Swimply still relies mostly on word of mouth to recruit new pool owners to its platform, Laskin said.
There's also potential to add more types of facilities to Swimply, Laskin said, from basketball courts to music studios.

title: 10 US Cities With the Largest Portion of Institutional Investors
summary:
If you've ever felt you can't compete with some offers when buying a home, you might be up against a collective effort from institutional investors.
"I think that people see institutional investors as a maybe strange or unnatural player in the housing market," Realtor.com senior economist Jake Krimmel told Business Insider.
"When it comes to the single-family market, this is a non-traditional actor, and the trend kind of started coming out of the Great Recession."
However, that hasn't stopped President Donald Trump from targeting large housing investors with a plan to ban them from buying single-family homes.
See where investors have the biggest share of single-family homes in the US.

title: Worried about a shaky stock market? This is what financial advisers suggest you do
summary:
Worried about a shaky stock market?
Stock markets have gone in the opposite direction, driven by uncertainty about the war's future and its effects on the economy.
Financial advisers say it depends on when you need to tap into your funds.
That's not to say a long period with a weak stock market is impossible.
It's classic advice to have a spread of holdings from the start, but when you are younger, those can be weighted toward the highly rewarding but fickle stock market.
